metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state via MongoDB (metadata) and file/object storage backends, exposes CRUD operations on S3 objects and buckets, and manages object lifecycle. Classified as stateful-crud.

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC, security groups, or network segmentation defined in any IaC
top_gaps.impact: Services lack network isolation; no blast radius containment

top_gaps.title: API Entry Point
top_gaps.gap: No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront in front of services
top_gaps.impact: No throttling, auth offload, or request validation at the edge

top_gaps.title: Auto-Scaling
top_gaps.impact: Cannot respond to traffic spikes; over-provisioning or outages likely

top_gaps.title: Backup and Recovery
top_gaps.gap: No backup configuration for MongoDB or any data store
top_gaps.impact: Data loss risk with no recovery mechanism

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging infrastructure

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 3) — GitHub Actions workflows with build, test, and deploy stages
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers deployments, checks build status across 7+ CI workflow jobs, and manages releases
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose workflow dispatch APIs and add structured build status reporting

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists — README.md, DESIGN.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, TESTING.md, CLAUDE.md, plus Sphinx docs
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A RAG-based knowledge agent that indexes existing documentation for developer questions
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index docs/ directory into a vector store; configure Bedrock for retrieval

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Structured logging and metrics in place (OPS-Q1 = 2) — Prometheus metrics, Grafana dashboards, werelogs
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that queries Prometheus metrics, correlates with logs, suggests root causes
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Add trace ID propagation; configure Bedrock for log analysis

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Identifiable modules with existing process separation (metadata server, data server) provide natural extraction boundaries

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 (monolith), INF-Q1=2 (limited managed compute), APP-Q3=3 but APP-Q4=2 (long-running processes sync)

triggering_questions.note: Monolith with identifiable modules but single deployable

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=2 but container definitions already exist (8 Dockerfiles, docker-compose); contextual guard prevents trigger

triggering_questions.note: Score below threshold but Dockerfiles exist — guard blocks

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Container definitions (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yaml) already exist. The workload is already containerized; the gap is in managed orchestration, not containerization itself.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=3 (minimal stored procedures); no commercial DB engines detected — MongoDB is open source

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold; no commercial engines detected

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. MongoDB is open source. No Oracle, SQL Server, or other licensed products found.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=2 (MongoDB and Redis self-managed in Docker containers)

triggering_questions.note: MongoDB and Redis self-managed with no managed service equivalents

immediate_actions: Update connection strings via env vars

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads or streaming infrastructure detected; contextual guard prevents trigger
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found. The application is an S3-compatible storage server, not a data processing workload.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=2 (partial IaC coverage), OPS-Q5=2 (no canary/blue-green deployment)

triggering_questions.note: Only monitoring has IaC; no production infrastructure defined
Image push only; no staged deployment strategy

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context describes an S3-compatible object-storage server with no mention of AI, agent, LLM, or ML use cases.

findings.title: No Network Security Infrastructure
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation configuration exists. All services bind to localhost or 0.0.0.0. Docker compose uses network_mode: host.
findings.gap: Complete absence of network security infrastructure — no private subnets, no security groups, no network segmentation between API, metadata, and data tiers.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets for database and application tiers. Place S3 API behind ALB in private subnets. Use security groups with least-privilege rules.

findings.title: No Managed API Entry Point
findings.description: S3 API is exposed directly on port 8000 with no gateway, load balancer, or CDN. All security and traffic control is application-level.
findings.gap: No managed entry point provides throttling, DDoS protection, request validation, or traffic management.
findings.recommendation: Deploy ALB in front of the service. For external-facing deployments, add CloudFront. Consider API Gateway for per-endpoint throttling.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling Configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling exists. Clusters config set to 1. No ASG, ECS scaling, HPA, or Lambda concurrency defined.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned. Cannot respond to traffic spikes or scale down during low demand.
findings.recommendation: Configure HPA in EKS based on request rate from existing Prometheus metrics on port 8002. Add cluster autoscaler for node-level scaling.

findings.title: No Backup and Recovery Configuration
findings.description: No backup configuration for MongoDB or any data store. No aws_backup_plan, backup_retention_period, or point-in-time recovery.
findings.gap: No automated backup or recovery mechanism. Data loss event would be unrecoverable from configuration in this repository.
findings.recommendation: Enable automated backups on DocumentDB with 7+ day retention and PITR. Implement S3 versioning for object data. Define AWS Backup plans in IaC.

findings.title: No Audit Logging Infrastructure
findings.description: No CloudTrail or infrastructure audit logging. Server access logs write to local filesystem without immutable storage or tamper protection.
findings.gap: No infrastructure-level audit trail. Server access logs lack immutability. No log file validation.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail for all AWS API calls. Ship server access logs to S3 with Object Lock. Configure CloudWatch Logs with defined retention.

findings.title: No SLO Definitions
findings.description: No formal SLOs defined. Prometheus alerts define thresholds but lack error budgets, SLO documentation, or measurement over time windows.
findings.gap: Alerting thresholds exist but no formal SLOs. Cannot measure whether service meets user expectations over time.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for critical S3 operations: availability (99.9%), p99 latency for GET/PUT/LIST/DELETE, error rate. Track error budgets.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, SSM Automation documents, or self-healing patterns exist. Alerts fire but response is entirely manual.
findings.gap: No incident response automation. No runbooks for common failure modes (MongoDB failover, Redis unavailability, backend timeout).
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common incidents. Implement self-healing with Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes and restart policies.

findings.title: Containerized Without Managed Orchestration
findings.description: Application is containerized (Docker, multi-stage build) but no managed orchestration (EKS/ECS/Fargate) is defined in IaC.
findings.gap: Containerized but no managed orchestration layer. Production deployment model is undefined in code.
findings.recommendation: Define EKS cluster infrastructure in Terraform/CDK. Create Kubernetes manifests for production deployment with resource limits, health checks, and scaling.

findings.title: Self-Managed Databases
findings.description: MongoDB configured as self-managed replica set in Docker. Redis self-managed via Docker container. No managed database services provisioned.
findings.gap: Both MongoDB and Redis are self-managed with no managed service equivalents (DocumentDB, ElastiCache).
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Amazon DocumentDB for metadata and ElastiCache for Redis. Define in IaC with Multi-AZ and automated backups.

findings.title: Workflow Orchestration Hardcoded
findings.description: Multi-step workflows (multipart upload, replication, lifecycle, KMS migration) are hardcoded application logic with no dedicated orchestration service.
findings.gap: Multi-step operations are hardcoded state machines without dedicated orchestration. Error handling and retry logic embedded in application code.
findings.recommendation: Adopt Step Functions for background workflows (replication, lifecycle). Keep latency-sensitive operations (multipart upload) in-process.

findings.title: No Managed Messaging Infrastructure
findings.description: Cross-service communication uses synchronous HTTP. Bucket notifications use dummy type. No SQS, SNS, EventBridge, or MSK defined.
findings.gap: State changes that trigger replication or lifecycle flow synchronously. Bucket notifications are non-functional (dummy type).
findings.recommendation: Adopt EventBridge for bucket event notifications. Use SQS for decoupling replication and lifecycle workflows from the API path.

findings.title: Limited High Availability Configuration
findings.description: MongoDB replica set provides data redundancy but no multi-AZ deployment defined in IaC. No load balancer distributes traffic.
findings.gap: Database has replica set pattern. Application layer has no multi-AZ or multi-instance HA defined.
findings.recommendation: Deploy across 2+ AZs in EKS with 2+ replicas. Configure DocumentDB Multi-AZ. Use ALB with cross-zone load balancing.

findings.title: Partial IaC Coverage
findings.description: IaC limited to monitoring (Prometheus alerts, Grafana dashboard) and CI (GitHub Actions, Docker compose). No production infrastructure in IaC.
findings.gap: Production infrastructure (compute, databases, networking, security, scaling) not defined in code.
findings.recommendation: Create Terraform/CDK modules for VPC, EKS, DocumentDB, ElastiCache, ALB, IAM, CloudWatch.

findings.title: Monolith with Identifiable Modules
findings.description: Single deployable container with clear module boundaries (api, auth, data, metadata, kms, management) and separate entry points but shared process.
findings.gap: Single deployable unit prevents independent scaling. Shared config and in-process dependencies create coupling.
findings.recommendation: Extract metadata and data services as independent containers on EKS using existing process separation.

findings.title: Long-Running Operations Handled Synchronously
findings.description: Object copy, SSE/KMS migration, and lifecycle transitions can exceed 30 seconds. Only multipart upload follows async pattern (S3 protocol).
findings.gap: Large object copy and lifecycle transitions lack async job tracking. SSE migration has 300s timeout with no status polling.
findings.recommendation: Implement async job processing with status polling for large copy and lifecycle operations. Use Step Functions or SQS workers.

findings.title: No API Versioning for Internal Extensions
findings.description: S3 protocol routes inherit AWS versioning (appropriate). CloudServer-specific extensions (Backbeat, management, health) have no version prefix.
findings.gap: Internal API extensions lack versioning. Breaking changes affect all consumers simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Add version prefixes to internal routes (/v1/backbeat/, /v1/management/). Document API compatibility.

findings.title: Operational Data on Local Filesystem
findings.description: Server access logs stored locally (/logs/server-access.log). Operational data in Docker volumes (localData/, localMetadata/).
findings.gap: Operational data lacks durability of managed object storage. No parsing pipeline for access logs.
findings.recommendation: Route server access logs to S3 with parsing pipeline. Store operational data in S3 for durability.

findings.title: Partial Encryption at Rest
findings.description: Object data encryption well-implemented via multiple KMS backends. MongoDB metadata and local storage volumes lack encryption at rest.
findings.gap: Object encryption exists but metadata in MongoDB and local volumes have no encryption. No customer-managed KMS key for infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Enable encryption at rest on DocumentDB with customer-managed KMS key. Enable EBS encryption for persistent volumes.

findings.title: Proprietary Identity Provider
findings.description: Identity managed through Scality Vault service rather than standard IdP. No OIDC/SAML federation. Fallback to static accounts in dev mode.
findings.gap: No standard centralized IdP integration. No SSO, no OIDC/SAML configured directly.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Cognito for user identity and OIDC federation. Replace Vault dependency with Cognito + IAM when deploying to AWS.

findings.title: Secrets in Environment Variables Without Rotation
findings.description: Docker secrets and env vars used. No Secrets Manager or rotation. Config has placeholder credentials (kmsAWS.ak: 'tbd'). Dev accounts committed.
findings.gap: No dedicated secrets management with rotation. Production credentials in env vars without encryption.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation for database credentials and backend access keys.

findings.title: Limited Compute Hardening
findings.description: Multi-stage Docker build with slim base. No vulnerability scanning on images. No hardened base (Bottlerocket/distroless). Dependency review PR-only.
findings.gap: No container image vulnerability scanning. Debian-based image rather than hardened alternative. No runtime patching strategy.
findings.recommendation: Add container scanning (Trivy/ECR) to CI. Consider Bottlerocket base. Implement Dependabot for automated dependency updates.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: Structured logging with request IDs (werelogs) and Prometheus metrics exist but no distributed tracing (X-Ray, OpenTelemetry) instrumented.
findings.gap: Cannot trace requests across S3 API → metadata → data → external backends. Request IDs not propagated as trace contexts.
findings.recommendation: Instrument OpenTelemetry SDK with X-Ray exporter. Propagate W3C traceparent between internal services.

findings.title: Infrastructure Metrics Only
findings.description: Prometheus metrics and UTAPI usage tracking exist but focus on operational data. No business-outcome metrics published to CloudWatch.
findings.gap: Missing business metrics: storage growth rate, data transfer costs, SLA adherence per customer, capacity planning signals.
findings.recommendation: Publish UTAPI metrics to CloudWatch. Add business metrics dashboards for per-account storage and API distribution.

findings.title: Static Threshold Alerting Only
findings.description: Prometheus alerts use fixed thresholds (error rate 3%/5%, latency per operation). No anomaly detection or dynamic baselines.
findings.gap: Cannot detect gradual degradation or novel failure modes. No composite alarms or multi-signal correlation.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudWatch anomaly detection on key metrics. Configure composite alarms for multi-signal alerting.

findings.title: No Staged Deployment Strategy
findings.description: Release workflow builds and pushes images to GHCR only. No canary, blue/green, or rolling deployment with health checks defined.
findings.gap: No staged deployment. Releases go directly to registry without progressive rollout or automated rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement canary deployments via EKS with Argo Rollouts. Start with rolling updates and health check gates.

findings.title: No AWS Resource Tagging Standard
findings.description: Docker image labels exist (git.repository, git.commit-sha). Prometheus labels defined. No AWS resource tagging strategy.
findings.gap: No tagging standard for AWS resources. When IaC is created, no governance in place.
findings.recommendation: Define tagging standard (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter) before IaC creation. Use default_tags in Terraform.

findings.title: CI/CD Lacks Deployment Stages
findings.description: Comprehensive CI (lint, test, build) via GitHub Actions. Release pushes images but no deployment automation to staging/production.
findings.gap: Build and test automated. Deployment limited to image push — no staging or production deployment automation.
findings.recommendation: Add deployment stages: staging deploy, integration test, promotion, canary/blue-green to production.

findings.title: AWS SDK v2 in Maintenance Mode
findings.description: Node.js 22 (modern runtime) but uses aws-sdk v2 (maintenance mode) rather than modular @aws-sdk v3.
findings.gap: AWS SDK v2 lacks tree-shaking, modern middleware, and TypeScript-first design of v3.
findings.recommendation: Migrate from aws-sdk v2 to @aws-sdk/* v3 modular packages. Consider gradual TypeScript adoption.

findings.title: Mix of Sync and Async Patterns
findings.description: Communication primarily sync HTTP between internal components. Some async: bucket notifications (dummy), Backbeat replication, WebSocket management.
findings.gap: State changes triggering replication/lifecycle flow synchronously. Bucket notifications non-functional.
findings.recommendation: Convert bucket notifications to EventBridge. Move replication triggers to SQS for decoupling.

findings.title: Environment Variable Service Configuration
findings.description: Endpoints configured via env vars and config.json. Docker entrypoint generates config from 40+ env vars. No dynamic service discovery.
findings.gap: Requires restart for endpoint changes. No dynamic discovery for failover or routing.
findings.recommendation: Leverage Kubernetes DNS in EKS. Adopt AWS Cloud Map or VPC Lattice for cross-cluster communication.

findings.title: Custom Authentication Implementation
findings.description: Strong AWS Sig V4 auth for S3 ops. Health check restricted by IP. Internal routes may have weaker auth patterns.
findings.gap: Custom-implemented auth rather than standard OAuth2/JWT. Internal service routes may lack strong mutual auth.
findings.recommendation: Add mTLS for internal service-to-service communication. Use Cognito authorizers for management endpoints behind API Gateway.

findings.title: SAST Present but Incomplete Pipeline
findings.description: CodeQL for SAST (JS, Python, Ruby). Dependency review on PRs. Missing container scanning, DAST, and blocking security gates.
findings.gap: No container image scanning, no DAST, no blocking gate on critical findings.
findings.recommendation: Add container scanning to build job. Configure CodeQL to block on critical findings. Add npm audit to CI.

findings.title: Mostly Centralized Data Access with Bypasses
findings.description: Primary data and metadata access is centralized through lib/metadata/ and lib/data/ abstractions supporting multiple backends. However, configuration data, auth data, and some operational queries bypass the main abstraction layers.
findings.gap: While primary data and metadata access is centralized, configuration data, auth data, and some operational queries bypass the main abstraction layers.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate remaining direct access patterns through the existing abstraction layers. Ensure all MongoDB access goes through the metadata abstraction rather than direct driver calls.

findings.title: Database Engine Versions Approaching Unpinned State
findings.description: MongoDB driver version is pinned (mongodb ^6.11.0) and compatible with MongoDB 6.0-8.0. Redis uses redis:alpine without explicit version pinning. No documented version-update procedure exists.
findings.gap: MongoDB server version not explicitly pinned in CI Docker image. Redis version unpinned. No documented version-update procedure covering downtime, rollback, and risk.
findings.recommendation: Pin MongoDB server version explicitly in CI compose. Pin Redis version. Document a version-update procedure. When migrating to DocumentDB, engine version will be managed by AWS.

findings.title: Minor MongoDB-Specific Query Patterns
findings.description: No stored procedures, triggers, or proprietary SQL. All business logic in the Node.js application layer. Some MongoDB-specific query patterns ($regex, $in) may need adjustment for DocumentDB compatibility.
findings.gap: Minor: some MongoDB-specific query patterns may need adjustment when migrating to DocumentDB, which has some MongoDB compatibility gaps.
findings.recommendation: Validate DocumentDB compatibility for existing MongoDB queries before migration. Test aggregation queries against DocumentDB's supported operations.

findings.title: Integration Tests Limited to Local Docker Environment
findings.description: Comprehensive functional test suites run in CI against local Docker environment (MongoDB, Redis, PyKMIP, storage backends). Coverage reported to Codecov with 80% patch target. Missing integration tests against deployed production-like infrastructure.
findings.gap: Integration tests exist and are comprehensive for local Docker testing. Missing: integration tests against deployed staging infrastructure (real DocumentDB, real ElastiCache, real EKS).
findings.recommendation: Add a staging integration test stage that runs the AWS SDK functional test suite against a deployed staging environment (EKS + DocumentDB + ElastiCache).

findings.title: Observability Assets Lack Explicit Ownership Attribution
findings.description: Monitoring directory contains maintained observability assets: Prometheus alerts, Grafana dashboard, dashboard generator, and alert test cases. No CODEOWNERS references observability assets, no named team/person ownership on alarms, no SLO attribution.
findings.gap: Observability assets exist and are maintained. Missing: explicit team/person ownership attribution on alarms, CODEOWNERS for monitoring/, SLO definitions tied to specific teams.
findings.recommendation: Add CODEOWNERS entries for monitoring/ directory. Add team attribution labels to Prometheus alerts. Define SLOs with team ownership for accountability.
